join_order.probe: orders(filtered by o_orderdate < 1995-03-15 via zonemap-pruned sequential scan)
join_order.notes: Materialize a dense bitset over customer keys from customer_segment_postings plus c_custkey, then test o_custkey during the sequential orders scan.

join_order.build: orders(filtered by customer bitset and o_orderdate < 1995-03-15)
join_order.notes: For each surviving order, traverse lineitem_order_postings and late-load l_shipdate, l_extendedprice, and l_discount only for that postings slice.

scan.table: customer(filtered via postings) -> orders(sequential zonemap-pruned scan) -> lineitem(postings expansion)
scan.access_pattern: sequential on orders blocks with zone-map pruning; late materialized random access on qualifying lineitem postings slices only

data_structures.aggregation: register_accumulator(per-order revenue in fused loop) + fixed_min_heap(10) per thread

indexes.purpose: enumerate BUILDING customer rowids without scanning customer
skip non-qualifying orders blocks before touching order payload columns
expand only qualifying orders to matching lineitems

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category C wasted work. The current implementation prefetches and advises roughly 2 GB of orders and lineitem data plus postings arrays up front, so hot time is dominated by data_loading rather than the fused revenue loop. Replace the customer-to-orders postings expansion with a compact customer-key bitset built from customer_segment_postings, run a zonemap-pruned sequential scan of orders to test both predicates in one pass, and only then probe lineitem_order_postings with late materialization of l_shipdate, l_extendedprice, and l_discount for qualifying order slices. This removes unnecessary upfront loading, preserves the fused order-to-lineitem pipeline, and keeps the working set proportional to surviving orders.
